name: The Sewer Scavenger
concept: You know the city from underneath — the drains, the crypts, the smugglers' tunnels, the Underway beneath the Black Cat itself. What the city throws away, you find; where the city cannot follow, you go. You have a stronger stomach and a longer memory for dark places than anyone alive should.
duty: The under-city keeps you fed and keeps the band's secrets. But there are older things down there than tunnels, and they are starting to notice you.

look.label: Bearing
options: eerily at home in the dark

look.label: Dress
options: layers against the damp and stink

look.label: Marks
options: scars from things that bit back
a permanent grime you've made peace with
a rash from the deep water
a lucky trinket dredged from the muck

gear.label: Kit
options: vials of the deep water's cures and poisons
chalk, string, and a nose for direction

gear.label: Guard
options: a thick oilskin that turns claws
a whistle only certain rats obey

moves.name: Down Below
moves.trigger: When you go into the drains, tunnels, crypts, and forgotten ways under the city.

results.success: You find the way through, or the thing you came for, and you endure the dark to do it.
results.failure: The under-city turns you around, or takes something from you for the passage.
advantage: Hold 1 Findings (spend: a hidden route to where you need to be / a valuable thing the muck was keeping / you sense the hazard before it reaches you / a bolt-hole to vanish into).
You learn what else has been using these ways lately.
threat: You bring something up with you, or leave a trail down for it to follow.
The deep water sickens you: a setback die until you can dry out and rest.
results.triumph: You find a way or a treasure no one else knows exists.
results.despair: You have woken, or been noticed by, something old and hungry below.

hold.name: Findings
spend: A hidden route brings you out exactly where you need to be.
You dredge up something valuable the muck was hiding.
You sense a hazard, a pit, or a lurker before it reaches you.
You know a bolt-hole to disappear into right now.

moves.name: Keeper of the Underway
moves.trigger: Because the map of the tunnels beneath the Black Cat lives in your head alone:
moves.text: You can move the band unseen under half the quarter, and hide what must not be found. Serve the den well this way and raise your Standing; lead something up the tunnels, or let the Underway be discovered, and lower it. What you gatekeep, you are also responsible for.

moves.name: Scrounge
moves.trigger: When you pick over a place — a wreck, a ruin, a rich man's midden — for what's worth taking.

results.success: You come away with something useful, valuable, or telling.
results.failure: There's nothing good here, and looking has cost you time or safety.
advantage: You find exactly the odd thing that solves a current problem.
You find it without anyone noticing you were here.
threat: What you found is claimed, cursed, or watched.
You disturbed something that will come looking.
results.triumph: You unearth a real prize, worth a season's rent.
results.despair: The treasure is bait, and you've sprung the trap.

moves.name: Cast-Iron Gut
moves.trigger: When filth, poison, disease, foul air, or sheer exhaustion would drop a softer body.

results.success: You shrug it off and keep going.
results.failure: Even you have a limit, and you've just found it.
advantage: Carry someone else through it too.
You know a cure or a trick to share.
threat: You endure it now, but it will catch up with you later.
results.triumph: You outlast a thing that should have killed you.
results.despair: It gets into you, and it does not let go easily.

moves.name: Never Lost
moves.trigger: When you need to find the way in the dark, the fog, or the maze of the under-city.

results.success: You know where you are and how to get where you're going.
results.failure: The way is stranger than you remember; something has changed down here.
advantage: Find the fastest route, or the safest, your pick.
Know where a pursuer will lose your trail.
threat: The route is open, but not empty.
results.triumph: You could walk it blind, and nearly have to.
results.despair: The map in your head is wrong now, and you don't know why.

moves.name: Rat-Friend
moves.trigger: When you deal with the vermin, strays, and half-wild things that rule the low places:
moves.text: Take a boost die to pass among them, read their alarm, or turn them from you. The rats of this city are cleverer and better-organized than anyone admits, and some of them are listening.

bonds: ____ came down into the dark with me once and did not scream; I trust them for it.
____ cannot stand the stink of me, and tries to hide it, and fails.
I showed ____ one secret way, and I watch to see if they keep it secret.
____ nursed me through a deep-water fever, and I have not forgotten.
I found something down below that belongs to ____, and I haven't decided whether to give it back.
communityTie: The Black Cat is safe because you keep the Underway; ____ is the only one who fully grasps what that costs you.
When the band must vanish, ____ trusts you to take them down and bring them up alive.
Something below has begun to take an interest in the den; ____ helps you keep watch on the dark.

tracks.name: Standing
tracks.note: How much the band relies on you and your hidden ways.

basic: Take a move from another playbook.
Take a move from another playbook.
Increase your maximum held Findings by 1.
Map a new deep: name a stretch of the under-city you now know cold.
Train a move you lean on: it upgrades 2 dice to proficiency (never more than its stat).
Raise your Standing by 1 by getting the whole band out of a trap through the tunnels.
advanced: Raise one stat by 1 (max 5). Rare, and only at a true turning point.
Go deep for good: follow the Underway to whatever waits at its end, and come back changed.
Claim the under-quarter: found a Bastion (a hidden warren) beneath the Black Cat that the band controls.
